questions.question: Which MGBE controller(s) on the module are wired to the QSFP cage / 25G path? (multi-select)

options.label: mgbe0
options.description: Thor MGBE0 (most common QSFP path on reference carriers)

questions.guidance: Check the carrier schematic — the QSFP cage's SerDes pair lands on exactly one MGBE controller. UPHY lane allocation for that controller is set at the platform/SKU level via the `uphy0-config-N` ODMDATA token (handled by /modify-uphy, not here). If the schematic ingest under <KB>/schematic/<carrier>_nets.json names the controller (e.g. `MGBE0_TX_*`/`MGBE0_RX_*` nets near the QSFP refdes), prefer that signal evidence.

questions.question: Per controller, which PHY mode should the kernel negotiate?
Per controller, is there an external board PHY in front of the cage, or is the SerDes wired directly to the QSFP cage?

options.label: phy
options.description: Board PHY between MGBE SerDes and the QSFP cage. Skill emits phy-handle, an `mdio { phy@<addr> { reg=<addr>; }; }` child node, and nvidia,phy-reset-gpios.

options.label: direct
options.description: MGBE SerDes wired straight to the QSFP cage (no MDIO PHY between). Skill omits phy-handle / mdio / phy-reset-gpios; phy-mode + nvidia,max-speed remain.

questions.question: Which I²C controller node is the PHY hung off? (only when phy_attach_kind == 'phy')
questions.guidance: Pick the I²C controller whose SDA/SCL nets reach the PHY refdes per the schematic. The node name is the unit-address of the I²C controller in the kernel DT — it appears under /bus@0/i2c@... at runtime.

questions.question: 7-bit I²C address of the PHY in hex (e.g. 0x56). (only when phy_attach_kind == 'phy')
questions.guidance: Read the PHY datasheet section on strap pins — the address is set by ADDR0..ADDR2 strap resistors at power-on. Cross-check against `i2cdetect -y -r <bus>` on a working reference board.

questions.question: Per controller, GPIO offset (decimal) of the PHY reset line within the chosen bank.
questions.guidance: The offset is the integer that appears in the DT phandle expression `<&gpio_main OFFSET 0>` (or `&gpio_aon`). Look up the Thor pinmap (<KB>/pinmap/<carrier>.json or the Thor SoC pinmux spreadsheet) for the exact gpio offset matching the carrier net name.

questions.question: Per controller, which GPIO bank holds the PHY reset line?
Which root `compatible` strings should this overlay match? (Pick at least one that matches the live DUT's /proc/device-tree/compatible — UEFI plugin-manager will skip the overlay otherwise.)
questions.guidance: Mirrors modify-camera Q-CON-3 / modify-pcie compatible_list. Verify with `cat /proc/device-tree/compatible | tr '\0' '\n'` on the booted DUT before answering. When session.platform_compatible_list is already populated (from a prior /custom-bsp clone or /modify-camera run), reuse those values rather than re-asking.
questions.ask_only_if: session.platform_compatible_list is empty AND no platform_defaults match
